facts.title: Algeria in Numbers
facts.sub: Fascinating facts about our amazing country
facts.journey.title: Algeria Journey at a Glance
gallery.title: Gallery of History
footer.desc: An educational adventure for the next generation
footer.fact.title: Quick Fact
footer.fact.text: Algeria is the 10th largest country in the world and the largest in Africa — covering 2.38 million km².
facts.geo.area.note: Largest country in Africa & 10th in the world
facts.geo.neighbors.note: Tunisia, Libya, Niger, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, W. Sahara
facts.hist.attacks.note: Simultaneous attacks on the first night
facts.eco.sonatrach.note: Largest company on the African continent
facts.cul.unesco.note: Including Tassili, Timgad, Djemila, Kasbah of Algiers
gallery.item.casbah.desc: UNESCO World Heritage Site - the historic heart of Algiers, a labyrinthine Ottoman medina
gallery.item.tassili.desc: Prehistoric art in the Sahara - the world's largest open-air rock art museum with 15,000+ paintings
gallery.item.timgad.desc: The Pompeii of Africa - remarkably preserved Roman colonial city in the Aures mountains
gallery.item.abdelkader.desc: The great Emir who led 15 years of resistance and built a functioning state against French occupation
gallery.item.nov1.desc: The night Algeria rose - 70 simultaneous attacks launched the revolution for independence
gallery.item.soummam.desc: In the mountains of Kabylia, the FLN organized itself into a true national liberation front
gallery.item.battlealg.desc: Urban guerrilla warfare in the Casbah that shocked the world and changed the course of the war
gallery.item.july5.desc: Independence Day - 132 years of colonization end as Algeria takes its rightful place among nations
gallery.item.ahaggar.desc: Volcanic peaks of the central Sahara - ancient, dramatic, and hauntingly beautiful
gallery.item.constantine.desc: The city of bridges - Constantine hangs dramatically over the Rhumel Gorge
gallery.item.ghardaia.desc: UNESCO-listed M'zab Valley - unique Ibadi architecture that inspired Le Corbusier himself
gallery.item.hirak.desc: Millions of Algerians peacefully marching every Friday for democracy
gallery.item.oases.desc: Over 250 oases dot the Algerian Sahara - green islands of life in the world's largest desert
gallery.item.djurdjura.desc: The backbone of Kabylia - rugged peaks, cedar forests, and the heart of Amazigh culture
gallery.item.fatma.desc: The Jeanne d'Arc of Kabylia - led fierce resistance against French forces in the mountains
gallery.item.oil.desc: February 24, 1971 - Algeria takes control of its natural resources, a historic economic milestone
home.fact.1: Algeria is home to the Barbary lion, once roaming its Atlas Mountains.
home.fact.2: Tassili n'Ajjer has 15,000+ cave paintings - the world's biggest open-air museum!
home.fact.3: Algeria is the LARGEST country in Africa and the 10th biggest in the whole world!
home.fact.4: Ibn Khaldoun wrote the world's very first sociology book back in 1377!
home.fact.5: The Roman ruins of Timgad are so well preserved they are called the Pompeii of Africa!
home.fact.6: Emir Abdelkader was so admired that a whole city in Iowa (USA) is named after him!
home.fact.7: Algeria has 1,200 km of beautiful Mediterranean coastline!
home.fact.8: On November 1, 1954, the FLN launched 70 simultaneous attacks to start the revolution!
